pattern: A hash map for O(1) lookup plus a recency-ordered structure for O(1) eviction together give a constant-time cache.
hints: Every get and put must be O(1), so you cannot scan to find the least-recently-used key — recency has to be maintained as you go.
You need two things at once: fast lookup by key, and fast identification + removal of the oldest entry.
Pair a dictionary with an ordering that moves a key to 'most recent' on every access; when size exceeds capacity, evict the front (oldest) entry.

edge_inputs.description: interleaved puts and gets with one eviction
capacity 1 evicts on every new key
a get counts as a use and protects a key from eviction
updating an existing key refreshes its recency without growing size

stress.description: long randomized get/put workload against a small capacity
stress.note: keys drawn from a small space so evictions and hits both occur
